page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Cache specifications include 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware, though the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a top-tier result in the PassMark CPU encryption test and strong ratings in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations. Single-core performance, including single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores, falls within the average tier. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D96ls_v5 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The absence of bundled local SSDs and GPUs reduces baseline resource overhead, making it a highly targeted choice for parallel processing tasks. It is well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 192.0 GB of system memory, representing a 2.0 GB per vCPU allocation. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in CPU encryption, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations, while single-core metrics remain average. This hardware configuration is highly cost-efficient for compute-intensive, parallelized workloads that do not demand high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance for parallel processing workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized VM with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, designed for high-throughput parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core-count, compute-optimized virtual machine designed for parallel processing, multi-threaded compilation, and high-throughput database operations.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors.
192.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Strong multi-core performance in CPU encryption, ray tracing, and database operations.
No bundled local storage or GPU, optimizing cost for pure compute workloads.
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and parallel data processing.
